questions.question: What's the biggest reason solo founders abandon CRMs?

options.text: Wrong tool choice
Over-engineering (too many fields) or under-using (glorified spreadsheet)
Lack of integrations
Cost

questions.explanation: Most CRM failures come from over-customization (too complex to maintain) or under-adoption (not using it consistently). Tool choice matters less than usage discipline.

questions.question: What's the universal 6-stage pipeline?

options.text: Prospect → Qualified → Demo → Proposal → Negotiation → Closed
Lead → Contacted → Engaged → Meeting → Proposal → Won/Lost
Awareness → Interest → Decision → Action → Retention → Advocacy
Cold → Warm → Hot → Meeting → Proposal → Won

questions.explanation: Lead → Contacted → Engaged → Meeting → Proposal → Won/Lost works universally because it maps to observable actions, not subjective judgments.

questions.question: Why is structured data critical for AI agents?

options.text: AI can't read free-text notes effectively
Structured fields enable reasoning, categorization, and prioritization
Dropdowns are faster to fill out than text fields
Both A and B

questions.explanation: AI agents need structured, categorical data to reason. Free-text notes are invisible to AI. Structured fields (dropdowns, numbers, dates) enable agents to prioritize, personalize, and recommend actions.

questions.question: What's the 'Would I Act on This?' test?

options.text: For every custom field: if the answer is no, delete it
For every automation: if it doesn't save time, turn it off
For every contact: if they're not ICP-fit, archive them
For every deal: if it's not moving, close it

questions.explanation: Every custom field must earn its place by triggering a decision or action. If you wouldn't act on the data, don't track it.

questions.question: What's the recommended weekly CRM hygiene routine?

options.text: Daily: log everything. Weekly: review pipeline. Monthly: dedupe.
Monday: pipeline review (15 min). Friday: hygiene sweep (15 min). Real-time: log meetings.
Weekly: update all deals. Monthly: full audit. Quarterly: migration.
Daily: check for new leads. Weekly: send follow-ups. Monthly: close lost deals.

questions.explanation: Monday pipeline review + Friday hygiene sweep + real-time meeting logging is the sustainable rhythm for solo founders. Daily CRM admin is overkill; monthly-only is too infrequent.

questions.question: Which CRM is best for high-volume outbound B2B sales?

options.text: Folk (relationship-first)
HubSpot Free (limited automation)
Close (built-in calling + sequences)
Attio (modern UX)

questions.explanation: Close is purpose-built for high-volume outbound with built-in calling, SMS, and sequences. Folk is for relationship selling. HubSpot Free lacks automation. Attio is great for AI-readiness but not volume-optimized.

questions.question: What's the #1 integration to verify on Day 7?

options.text: Calendar sync
Email sync (sent and received emails log automatically)
Enrichment (Apollo, Clearbit)
Zapier/Make automations

questions.explanation: Email sync is the foundation. Without it, your CRM has amnesia. Calendar, enrichment, and automations are important but secondary to email logging.

questions.question: What's the AI-Readiness Score range for 'Ready for Course 27'?
questions.explanation: 7-8 means full AI-ready schema deployed, structured event logging, health indicators present, and 3+ automations running. That's the threshold for building AI agents in Course 27.

questions.question: What happens if you delay CRM setup by 1 week after choosing a tool?

options.text: Nothing — you can set it up anytime
Adoption drops 60% for every week of delay
You forget which tool you chose
Your free trial expires

questions.explanation: Research shows CRM adoption drops 60% for every week of delay after the purchase decision. The 48-hour window is real.

questions.question: What's the primary output artifact of Course 40?

options.text: CRM Comparison Scorecard
Pipeline Stage Map
AI-Ready Field Schema
Complete CRM Setup Checklist (compiles all lesson artifacts)

questions.explanation: The Complete CRM Setup Checklist is the culminating artifact that compiles your CRM philosophy, tool choice, pipeline, schema, integrations, hygiene routine, and AI-readiness score.
